NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PREFERENCE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(A) (TRANSACTION ID # 76182361) FILED BY PLAINTIFF HARREDEN, ARTHUR W. HEARING SET FOR MAY-27-2025 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 304

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 304, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF IS GRANTED UNDER C.C.P. 36(A). THE TRIAL DATE IS SEPTEMBER 15, 2025, AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 206. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 36(F) IS SEPTEMBER 24, 2025. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS IS SEPTEMBER 9, 2025. TIME TO RESPOND TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY NOT YET SERVED IS SHORTENED TO 20 DAYS. THE FACT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS AUGUST 29, 2025. THE EXPERT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS SEPTEMBER 12, 2025. ALL BANKRUPTCY DOCUMENTS SHALL BE TURNED OVER TO DEFENDANTS NO LATER THAN JUNE 26, 2025. A FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR JULY 29, 2025, AT 4:00 P.M. A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 11, 2025, AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. PERSONAL APPEARANCE IS REQUIRED BY ALL UNLESS A PARTY IS EXCUSED FOR GOOD CAUSE. A REQUEST TO EXCUSE A PERSONAL APPEARANCE MUST BE SUBMITTED TO DEPARTMENT503@SFTC.ORG TWO WEEKS BEFORE THE MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN. CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Jul-28-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preference Pursuant To C.C.P. 36(A)
Off Calendar May-27-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 27, 2025, in Department 304, Line 1.
No appearances, not contested, The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. 36(a). Opposition filed by Defendant Harris Corporation. Reply filed.
Defendant's opposition is meritless. Defendant in its opposition requests that the court set the trial date no sooner than 120 days from the hearing and impose certain conditions on discovery and pre-trial motions. However, the court already adheres to the practice of scheduling trial dates as close to 120 days as possible. Moreover, the court will not impose conditions on Plaintiffs' right to a preferential trial date. "Where a party meets the requisite standard for calendar preference under [Code of Civil Procedure section 36] subdivision (a), preference must be granted. No weighing of interests is involved." (Fox v. Superior Court (2018) 21 Cal.App.5th 529, 535; see also, e.g., Miller v. Superior Court (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 1200, 1204 [statute "grants a mandatory and absolute right to trial preference"]; Swaithes v. Superior Court (1989) 212 Cal.App.3d 1082, 1085 [trial court "has no power to balance the different interests of opposing litigants in applying the provision"]; Koch-Ash v. Superior Court (1986) 180 Cal.App.3d 689, 694 [section 36(a) "must be deemed to be mandatory and absolute" and "no discretion is left to trial courts."].)

1. The trial date is September 15, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is September 24, 2025.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20.
2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is September 9, 2025.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.
3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter.
4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is August 29, 2025.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is September 12, 2025.
7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than June 26, 2025.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

(mini-minute continues 1 of 2, see next entry)
